response.justification: Strong geospatial domain expertise through GISCollective project (used in production by Green Map) and exceptional testing/QA culture align with the role's needs, but modern C++ skills—the primary technical requirement—are notably absent. Last C++ work was ~10 years ago at National Instruments; recent career (2021-present) has diverged into TypeScript/Node.js. Demonstrated ability to learn diverse tech stacks could mitigate this gap, but significant upskilling would be required.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain expertise through GISCollective project, currently used in production with direct navigation/mapping experience
Strong testing and quality assurance culture with TDD practice, custom test libraries, and structured QA training creation
Proven ability to master new technology stacks and work across embedded, backend, frontend, and infrastructure domains
concerns: Modern C++ experience is missing; last C++ work was ~10 years ago at National Instruments, not recent commercial systems programming
Recent career trajectory (2021-present) focused on TypeScript/Node.js and high-level development, diverging from systems-level programming
No Rust experience mentioned, which would serve as an acceptable alternative to C++ per job requirements

key_factors.factor: Geospatial domain expertise and shipping products
Absence of modern C++ or Rust experience
Testing-first development culture and QA mindset
